posts.author: HighCAGR
posts.community: V2 Retail - Second innings playing out well
posts.text: Thanks for sharing the framework, buddy, it’s really insightful. Really enjoyed reading, and in my limited understanding, its interesting to track the shift from store opening story to store maturation story. One thing that stood out to me from the management commentary was their apparent focus on sales productivity and inventory velocity over maximizing gross margins, which reminded me somewhat of Zudio’s approach. I also keep coming back to management’s comment that mature stores derive 68% revenue from repeat customers. To me, that feels like one of the strongest indicators of whether a sto…

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: V2 Retail - Second innings playing out well
posts.text: C07CF33F-2162-4098-9583-02EFA79A43FE_1_105_c 904×868 195 KB Thank you — this is exactly the right variable to question. The honest answer is that V2 does not yet publish a clean vintage-wise store cohort table, so we cannot prove the maturity curve with hard store-level data yet. But management has given one useful internal benchmark. Newer stores are judged against older-store productivity, and the benchmark is that they should be within roughly 30% of older-store sales PSF. The nuance is important: management has not said every older store is doing ₹1,200 PSF/month. The better reading is tha…

posts.text: is Zepto, Blinkit, Instamart, Jiomart a competition for this? Jiomart is expanding delivery to villages and small towns as well.

posts.author: HighCAGR
posts.community: V2 Retail - Second innings playing out well
posts.text: UdayChandak Firstly, thank you for putting together and sharing this research. What I appreciated most was that the thesis evolved from simply describing the business to defining a clear decision-making framework. The sections on V2 is still on trial, store economics, and the decision ledger were particularly useful because they focused on what actually matters for future returns rather than just celebrating past growth. One question that I think sits at the heart of the investment case: If V2 is now a store-maturation story rather than a store-opening story, do you have any data points, chann…

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: V2 Retail - Second innings playing out well
posts.text: V2 Retail and the Architecture of Affordable Aspiration The visible story is store growth. The deeper one is whether each new location becomes part of local routine, customer trust, and repeat demand. Opening Line The best neighborhood businesses do not win by being extraordinary. They win by becoming the choice people stop rethinking. That is one of the most underrated forms of economic power: becoming a low-friction decision. Think of the pharmacy at the corner you walk into when you do not want to compare five options. The restaurant on a highway you choose because you know the stop will no…
